pages.raw_text:
The Building Trades Alliance
placed the Smyth Plumbing Co. on
the unfair list last Tuesday evening
at the request of the plumbers
union, who demand that the firm
adopt the closed shop policy. No
attempt will be made to coerce the
company until all present contracts
are completed, but a fight will be
made against the firm on all new
work undertaken.


The hupture between the plumb
ing concern and the union is the
result of the fight made by the
union against the style of plumbing
installed in the Columbia block.
The union protested against the
plumbing and called out the men
in the employ of the Smyth COM
pany. A number of the men re-
fused to quit and remained at work
and were largely instrumental in
beating the union in this particu-
lar instance. The company has
been able to secure several non
union steamfitters and plumbers to
fill out their force of men, but until
the present time have not been
molested for using nonunion men.


James Smyth, of the Smyth
Plumbing Co., said that he had not
been informed of the action of the
alliance and he refused to discuss
the matter, saying he preferred to
wait until the fight was started on
his new contracts.
